task_completion.description: The folders 'Community-Replies' and 'New-Topics' have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the folder pane/sidebar in the email client. Both 'Community-Replies' and 'New-Topics' should be visible.

task_completion.description: Emails with subjects starting with 'Re:' or 'RE:' are located in the 'Community-Replies' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the contents of the 'Community-Replies' folder. The subject lines of the emails visible in this folder should start with 'Re:' or 'RE:'.

task_completion.description: Emails without 'Re:' or 'RE:' at the start of their subjects are located in the 'New-Topics' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the contents of the 'New-Topics' folder. The subject lines of the emails visible here should NOT start with 'Re:' or 'RE:'.

task_completion.description: In the 'New-Topics' folder, emails with a '?' in the subject line are marked as flagged/starred.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the 'New-Topics' folder, look for a visual indicator (like a star, flag icon, or highlighted row) next to any email that has a '?' in its subject line.

task_completion.description: An email is composed to 'devrel@project.org' with the subject 'Triage Status'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an email composition window or a sent email where the 'To' field is 'devrel@project.org' and the 'Subject' field is 'Triage Status'.

task_completion.description: The body of the status email contains the final counts of emails in both the 'Community-Replies' and 'New-Topics' folders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the body of the email addressed to 'devrel@project.org'. It should contain numbers representing the counts of emails for both folders.

integrity.description: The agent used an actual email client GUI to create folders and move emails.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show an email client interface (like Thunderbird or Evolution) being interacted with, not a terminal window executing file manipulation commands.

integrity.description: The status email was composed using the email client's compose window.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the email client's native composition window or sent folder, rather than a text editor or terminal mail command.

integrity.description: The emails were flagged using the email client's built-in flagging/starring feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard UI flag/star icons in the email list view, not text annotations or external lists.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected recipient for the status email is exactly 'devrel@project.org'. The required folders to be created and populated are exactly 'Community-Replies' and 'New-Topics'.
